one thing I would love to do is to be able to hold all ground truths, meaning, everything the user has ever said about the project, or agreed about the project and load it all in the context window of a large model that has a big context window and ask it questions, or refine it, or have it recompile the entire wiki or whatever -- how can we approach that? Review comprehensively and give me some ideas.

Proposed a `pc oracle` subcommand with modes for full-wiki Q&A, interactive REPL, recompile, and gap detection, plus a future pure-user-claim export. Recommended building the basic Q&A mode first as highest value.
Open all this as a GitHub issue to explore in the future

Assess if the following makes sense, if it does, create a github issue in this repo for it. ARGUMENTS: Add a `pc oracle` subcommand that loads all ground truths (wiki guides + eventually user-origin claims from the citation log) into a single large-context model call, enabling three modes: 1. **`pc oracle <question>`** — concatenates all wiki guides (~100K tokens) and sends them with the question to a big-context model, streaming the answer. No routing, no embedding lookup — raw context Q&A. Example: `pc oracle "What did I decide about the capture routing bottleneck?"` 2. **`pc oracle --interactive`** — REPL mode that keeps the full wiki loaded as persistent context across multiple turns. 3. **`pc oracle --recompile`** — sends all guides in a single call to identify contradictions, merge near-duplicates, fill gaps, and writes revised guides back to disk. Handles citation stability by either re-anchoring citations or restricting recompilation to citation-free guides. This is a global-consistency pass the current pairwise wiki-doctor can't do. 4. **`pc oracle --gaps`** — gap detection pass: sends full wiki with "what topics are missing or underdefined?" — the one-shot rebuild eval run as a structured audit. **Future layer**: once `_citations.log` gets `author` tags (planned in the event-sourced claim log redesign), add a `--ground-truth` flag that uses only user-origin citation entries instead of the synthesized wiki guides — the purest "everything the user has ever said" corpus. **Implementation notes:** - Walk `wiki_dir()`, concatenate all `*.md` files - Build prompt: `[WIKI CONTEXT]
{all_guides}
[QUESTION]
{question}` - Send via existing provider abstraction, stream to stdout - Model selection matters: want `gemini-2.0-flash` or `claude-opus-4-8` for large-context quality - ~80–120K tokens total for the full wiki — fits in 200K context easily
Confirmed the plan makes sense and created GitHub issue #1 for the `pc oracle` feature.
